200.q: I have hands but cannot clap. I have a face but no eyes. What am I?
I am tall when young and short when old. You burn me to light the dark. What am I?
I have keys but no locks. I have space but no room. You can enter but not go inside. What am I?
The more you take, the more you leave behind. What am I?
I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. I have no body but I come alive with wind. What am I?
What has four legs but doesn't walk?
What must be broken before you can use it?
The more I dry, the wetter I get. What am I?
What goes up but never comes down?
I have cities but no houses. I have mountains but no trees. I have water but no fish. What am I?
I have an eye but cannot see. I am small and pointy. What am I?
I follow you all day but disappear when night falls. What am I?
I have a head and a tail but no body. What am I?
I have a neck but no head. I may be filled with drink. What am I?
I have many teeth but cannot bite. I help neaten your hair. What am I?
I can travel around the world while staying in a corner. What am I?
I run but never walk. I have a bed but never sleep. What am I?
I have a thumb and four fingers but I am not alive. What am I?
I am full of holes but still hold water. What am I?
I have roots no one sees. I am taller than trees. Up up I go and yet never grow. What am I?
I am always in front of you but can never be seen. What am I?
I have branches but no fruit, trunk, or leaves. You visit me to hold your money. What am I?
The more you have of me, the less you see. What am I?
I have one head, one foot, and four legs. Each night you sleep on me. What am I?
I am so fragile that saying my name breaks me. What am I?
Until I am measured, I am not known, yet you miss me when I have flown. What am I?
I have teeth but cannot eat. I keep clothes closed. What am I?
I can fly without wings and cry without eyes. I drift across the sky. What am I?
I have 13 hearts but no organs. I am shuffled and dealt. What am I?
I grow short the more I am used. I have wood but am not a tree. What am I?

400.q: I am lighter than a feather, yet the strongest person cannot hold me for long. What am I?
I can hide a whole city, but I am made of tiny drops. The more of me there is, the less you see. What am I?
What can you catch but not throw?
I can be cracked, told, or played. People laugh when they hear me. What am I?
What 5-letter word becomes shorter when you add two letters to it?
What belongs to you, but other people use it more than you do?
I have many keys but open no locks. I make music instead. What am I?
What has four wheels and flies?
What can two people have without ever touching it?
I am the beginning of everything and the end of everywhere. What am I?
A five-letter word that sounds the same after removing four of its letters. What is it?
Give me food and I will live. Give me water and I will die. What am I?
I have no voice but teach you much. I have spines and hinges but am not a plant or a door. What am I?
What begins with the letter e and ends with the letter e but only contains one letter?
What comes once in a minute, twice in a moment, but never in a thousand years?
What invention lets you look right through a wall?
You can see me in water, but I never get wet. What am I?
I begin and have no end. I am also the ending of all that begins. What am I?
I am a word of 5 letters. People eat me. Remove my first letter and I become a form of energy. What am I?
People buy me to eat but never eat me. What am I?
If you have me, you want to share me. If you share me, you don't have me. What am I?
What kind of tree can you carry in your hand?
What room do ghosts avoid?
I am not a plant but I have leaves. I am not royalty but I have a crown. I am sweet but sugar was never added. What am I?
You can hear me but never see me. I come out when you speak. What am I?
I can run but have no legs. I am on your face. What am I?
What invention allows you to walk through walls?
One of us breaks yet never falls. The other falls yet never breaks. What are we?
I keep ships from drifting. I sink but never drown. What am I?
I have an eye but cannot see. I can tear roofs from homes. What am I?
I grow when fed but die when watered. What am I?

600.q: Three lives have I. Gentle enough to soothe the skin, light enough to caress the sky, hard enough to crack rocks. What am I?
Two bodies have I, though both joined in one. The more still I stand, the quicker I run. What am I?
At night they come without being fetched, and by day they are lost without being stolen. What are they?
The man who makes me does not want me. The man who buys me does not use me. The man who uses me does not know it. What am I?
Always old, sometimes new. Never sad, sometimes blue. Never empty, sometimes full. What am I?
I make you weak at the worst of times. I make your heart pound and hands sweat. I visit the weak but seldom the bold. What am I?
A cloud is my mother, the wind is my father. A rainbow is my bed, the earth my final resting place. What am I?
I am the black child of a white father, a wingless bird flying even to the clouds of heaven. What am I?
He marries many women but has never been married himself. Who is he?
Alive without breath, as cold as death. Never thirsty, ever drinking. All in mail, never clinking. What am I?
I start with T, end with T, and have T in me. What am I?
What word in the dictionary is spelled incorrectly?
Voiceless it cries, wingless flutters, toothless bites, mouthless mutters. What is it?
I can be long or short. I can be grown or bought. I can be painted or left bare. I can be round or square. What am I?
The sun bakes them, the hand breaks them, the foot treads on them, and the mouth tastes them. What are they?
I turn once — that which is outside will not get in. I turn again — that which is inside will not get out. What am I?
I am the fountain from which no one can drink. Like gold to all I am sought. What am I?
What can fill a room but takes up no space?
I have no life, but I can die. You plug me in to charge. What am I?
What has three feet but cannot walk?
Poor people have it. Rich people need it. If you eat it, you die. What is it?
The more you take away from me, the bigger I grow. What am I?
I have a tongue but cannot taste, a sole but no soul. What am I?
What are the next two letters in the series: O, T, T, F, F, S, S, _, _?
A man rode into town on Friday, stayed three days, and left on Friday. How?
A mother had three children. The first was named April, the second May. What was the third child's name?
What is black when clean and white when dirty?
I get smaller every time I take a bath. What am I?
I am always coming but never arrive. What am I?
I have 64 squares, 32 pieces do battle on me. What am I?
What has many teeth, holds a grip, and lives in fabric and leather?
I am always hungry and must be fed. The finger I touch will soon turn red. What am I?
